message:
fix(db/sanitize): re-strip SQL comment markers after FTS5 metachar removal

The FTS5 metachar strip in sanitize_fts_query can inadvertently create
new SQL comment markers when stripping brackets/braces between adjacent
punctuation. For example, input "/[*" becomes "/*" after removing "[",
and "-[-" becomes "--" after removing "[". Both are SQL comment markers
that the initial stripping pass already scrubbed.

Proptest prop_fts_sanitize_no_match_operators caught this via the
minimal failing input "/[*ª". Fix: run the "--", "/*", "*/" strip loops
a second time after the metachar retain, then re-collapse whitespace.

Two passes are sufficient: comment-marker stripping replaces with
spaces, which cannot introduce new dashes or slash/star pairs, so no
third-order reformation is possible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
